justifications.completeness: Missed all four pedestrians and reported only two of five vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Included motion and door/trunk attributes for some vehicles and a scene description but omitted attributes for the van and many objects, so attribute coverage is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relative descriptions for the two detected vehicles which may be correct, but failed to describe spatial relations for the majority of vehicles and all pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a couple of vehicles with some attributes but misses most objects and details, yielding low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Misses all three pedestrians and most vehicles, only detects one truck, so largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes for one vehicle and a simple scene description but lacks detailed person actions and multiple vehicle entries present in ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a left-side relation for the truck but omits spatial context for pedestrians and many other vehicles, limiting spatial coverage.
justifications.overall: Overall extraction captures a single vehicle with limited detail while missing pedestrians and most vehicles, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses all four pedestrians and five of six annotated vehicles, reporting only a single vehicle and a generic scene description.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some scene-level description and a few vehicle attributes but omits pedestrian actions, ages, transport status, and most vehicle instances and states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a spatial relation for one vehicle but fails to localize any pedestrians or the majority of vehicles relative to the scene.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures minor descriptive details but is sparse and incomplete, missing critical objects and semantic labels from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures a single vehicle but omits all six pedestrians and most vehicles present in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: While some vehicle-level attributes are reported (type, motion status, trunk/doors), the model misses most object instances and all detailed pedestrian attributes and actions, giving limited semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation for the truck is provided and may be correct, but no spatial relations are reported for pedestrians or other vehicles, so spatial information is sparse.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and incomplete — it contains a few correct vehicle attributes but misses the majority of objects and critical pedestrian information, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 12 ground-truth entities (one pedestrian and one vehicle), missing most people and vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several attribute fields (action, context, transporting, age) for the single detected pedestrian and vehicle but misses attributes for all other objects and misattributes transporting in context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides spatial relations for only one pedestrian and the truck, but fails to localize other pedestrians and vehicles and overgeneralizes positions.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and only partially correct in attributes and spatial cues, yielding limited utility for perception evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, so coverage of entities is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes for one pedestrian and a scene description but omits many entities and contextual attributes (e.g., transporting details, communicative actions) present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only supplies a single vague relation near the storefront and fails to capture important relations like on-road, jaywalking, or vehicle motion/parking statuses.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, missing most entities and many key relations and motion states from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Only one of seven pedestrians and one of three vehicles were reported, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction captures basic attributes like atomic action and age for a single pedestrian and vehicle states but misses many contextual, communicative, and transporting labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial label was provided and vehicle relations were omitted, failing to reflect multiple pedestrians described as on the road, along the side, or jaywalking.
justifications.overall: Due to large omissions in detected agents and missing contextual and spatial details the extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Most critical entities are missing; the model omitted all seven pedestrians and four vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a basic scene description about buildings and signage but completely lacks action labels, ages, transport states, and vehicle motion/door states from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model correctly indicates left and right building placement yet fails to capture any spatial relations or positions of pedestrians and vehicles on the road.
justifications.overall: While some superficial scene details are correct, the near-total omission of pedestrians and vehicles makes the extraction largely inadequate for autonomous driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one vehicle and no pedestrians while ground truth contains four pedestrians and five vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic vehicle attributes but omits pedestrian actions, multiple vehicle instances, and many contextual details, giving low semantic richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a single spatial relation for one parked car but fails to describe pedestrian locations, jaywalking, or other vehicles' positions, so spatial relations are poor.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction misses most objects and key semantics, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing several objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes are inconsistently reported with several incorrect labels (wrong ages, incorrect transporting, and mismatched communicative acts) despite some correct atomic actions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are sparse and partly incorrect, missing jaywalking and many road-relative relations and providing only one 'right side' relation.
justifications.overall: Low recall and inconsistent attribute extraction lead to a poor overall result despite a few correct atomic action labels.

justifications.completeness: Only one of four pedestrians and one of four vehicles were extracted, so most annotated objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction captures basic attributes (action, age, door/trunk state) but omits object IDs, important contextual labels like jaywalking, and mislabels vehicle motion, reducing semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation is provided for one pedestrian while spatial contexts for other pedestrians and relative locations of vehicles are omitted.
justifications.overall: The output is sparse and partially incorrect, missing most agents and key contextual labels, so its overall usefulness for driving perception is low.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian matching a ground truth entry but missed three other pedestrians and all three vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several attributes for the detected pedestrian but lacks labels for jaywalking and vehicle states present in the ground truth and misses multiple entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a single right side relation for one pedestrian but omits spatial relations for other pedestrians and all vehicles and does not capture jaywalking positions.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one correct pedestrian with some attributes but is largely incomplete and misses vehicle detections and critical contextual labels, yielding low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The model only labeled the scene as a road with an urban description but missed all five pedestrians and three vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is almost entirely missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: No semantic attributes such as actions, ages, motion status, or object states were reported, providing effectively no semantic detail about detected entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction contains no information about positions or spatial relations of agents relative to the road or each other, so spatial context is absent.
justifications.overall: Because the extraction missed all dynamic agents and their attributes despite a minimal scene description, the overall quality is nearly a complete failure.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and one of two vehicles, missing multiple actors and thus providing low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted several attribute types (actions, age, door/trunk states) for detected objects but failed to capture most actors and misreported vehicle motion, limiting semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided a single spatial relation for one pedestrian while omitting positional context for other actors and misaligning vehicle motion, so spatial understanding is weak.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures some correct attributes for a single pedestrian but misses most actors and vehicle motion, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and no vehicles while the ground truth contains eight persons and one vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected person the model captured multiple attributes like action, context, and age, but it omitted many other actors and finer labels such as jaywalking and vehicle state.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation was provided and the extraction failed to represent the varied on road versus sidewalk and jaywalking relations present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows some attribute-level detail for one person but misses most actors and the vehicle, resulting in poor overall coverage and situational understanding.

justifications.completeness: Missed one of two pedestrians and the majority of vehicles present, so many objects are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like motion status, trunk and doors, and age, but misses key behaviors such as biking and jaywalking and omits several vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial tags but they are coarse and incomplete, and the pedestrian relation is imprecise compared to ground truth.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few correct attributes but is incomplete and spatially imprecise, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Misses one person who is biking and all vehicles present, and fails to label jaywalking, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes for one pedestrian but misses other actors and key labels like biking and vehicle states, reducing semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers a single spatial relation for one pedestrian which may be incorrect and omits relations for the other person and multiple vehicles.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one pedestrian with limited detail but omits another person and all vehicles and contains context errors, leading to low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses both pedestrians and two of the three annotated vehicles, reporting only a single vehicle so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: While it provides a few vehicle attributes (motion_status, trunk/doors), it omits all person-level attributes and many vehicle instances and contextual details.
justifications.spatial_relations: It provides a single incorrect spatial relation (inside the vehicle) and fails to capture relations for pedestrians or parked vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incorrect and incomplete with missing objects and an erroneous spatial relation, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only a generic scene context and missed all annotated persons and vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided only a brief scene description and no entity-level attributes such as actions, ages, or motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included a general layout (buildings on both sides) but failed to report spatial relations between the road and the annotated people or vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and lacks semantic detail and spatial relations, capturing only a high-level scene description.

justifications.completeness: Extraction mentions a woman riding a bicycle in the scene description but fails to list any pedestrian or the six vehicles present in the ground truth, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides only a coarse scene description and a mention of a cyclist with no object-level attributes (e.g., action labels, age, vehicle motion or door/trunk states) from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a simple spatial cue ('left side of the road') for the cyclist but supplies no spatial relations for the multiple vehicles or detailed placements.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is shallow and incomplete: it captures a single brief scene detail but omits required object enumerations and rich semantic attributes from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Lists only two pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth contains seven persons and three vehicles, missing most entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few attributes but includes incorrect age and communicative labels and omits many object-level properties and behaviors present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single sidewalk relation is provided and the extraction fails to capture multiple accurate spatial contexts like walking along the road, crossing at the pedestrian crossing, and vehicle locations.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partially inaccurate across entity detection, attributes, and spatial relations, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and zero vehicles while ground truth contains eight people and three vehicles, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (atomic action, transporting, age) for a single pedestrian but misses most actors, contains a context typo (uses 'pedal' instead of 'pedestrian') and hallucinates a traffic officer, so semantic detail is limited and partly incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: The only spatial relation ('in front of traffic officer') is unsupported by the ground truth and no relations to vehicles or other pedestrians are provided, making spatial information largely incorrect.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and contains hallucinations despite a few correct per-person attributes, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth contains 7 persons and a parked vehicle, so many entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are provided (actions, context, transporting) but several are incorrect or inconsistent (wrong ages, incorrect communicative labels) and many per-person attributes are missing or mismatched.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only one vague relation referencing a man directing traffic is given (not present in the ground truth) while important relations to the crosswalk, jaywalking pedestrians, and the parked vehicle are absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small subset of the scene with mixed attribute accuracy and poor spatial coverage, yielding limited overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth contains seven persons and four vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided some attribute fields (age, transporting, communicative) for the single detected person but misclassified the action and omitted many per-person attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly identified 'in the middle of the road' for the detected person but failed to capture other spatial placements (along the side, on the road) for multiple persons.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partially inaccurate, missing most entities and several attribute details, leading to low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected the crosswalk location but omitted all annotated pedestrians and vehicles, thus capturing very little of the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides only a high-level scene description and no object-level attributes, actions, or transport states present in the annotations.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships or relative positions between persons, vehicles, or scene elements were reported.
justifications.overall: Extraction fails to report nearly all required entities and attributes, offering only a vague and partially incorrect scene description.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists eight persons and four vehicles, so most relevant objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes attributes like actions, context, and transporting but mislabels age for one person, omits many persons and vehicle attributes, and adds an unverified traffic officer, indicating partial but inaccurate semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only one pedestrian has spatial relations provided and they are coarse while other pedestrians and all vehicles lack spatial relation annotations, so spatial information is sparse and incomplete.
justifications.overall: Due to major omissions of persons and all vehicles, some incorrect attribute labels, and sparse spatial data, the extraction quality is poor and insufficient for reliable perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains five persons and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like age and a vehicle motion state are provided but many action/context labels are incorrect or omitted (e.g., multiple standing/walking/pushing labels missing).
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single pedestrian spatial relation is given and vehicle spatial relations are empty, failing to reflect several road/sidewalk relations present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct elements but is largely incomplete and has limited and partially incorrect semantics and spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth contains five people and four vehicles, so many entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few attributes for one person but misses transporting labels, several distinct actions, and all vehicle properties, yielding low semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reports on the sidewalk for the single pedestrian while ground truth indicates multiple pedestrians on the road or along the side, so spatial relations are often incorrect.
justifications.overall: Many entities and attributes are missing or incorrect, so overall extraction is poor.

justifications.completeness: Only one of five annotated persons was reported and no vehicles were detected, so most actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (action, context, age) were provided for a single pedestrian but many actions like standing and pushing and vehicle attributes are absent.
justifications.spatial_relations: A plausible spatial relation was given for one person but spatial relations for the other pedestrians and any vehicles are missing or inconsistent with annotations.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a single pedestrian reasonably well but is otherwise incomplete and misses most semantic and vehicle information.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all annotated pedestrians and the parked vehicle, capturing only some environmental elements.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the scene description includes details like construction cones, a turn lane, and a restaurant sign, it omits critical semantic entities and actions such as multiple walking people, a person pushing, and a parked car.
justifications.spatial_relations: The VLM correctly notes left-side construction and trees and buildings on both sides, but fails to report spatial relations of pedestrians and the parked vehicle relative to the road.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides some background scene detail but is severely lacking in essential traffic participants and their behaviors, making it inadequate for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian and no vehicles were reported despite six people and multiple vehicles in the ground truth, so most actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several attribute fields (atomic action, transporting, age) but many labels are incorrect or absent relative to the ground truth and two pedestrians with 'pushing' were omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported relation 'being directed by traffic officer' is not supported by the ground truth and no correct spatial relations to vehicles or other pedestrians were provided.
justifications.overall: The output captures a small, partly detailed fragment of the scene but misses most actors, vehicles, and several key attributes, resulting in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is reported out of ten in the ground truth and no vehicles are detected, so the majority of scene entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies some attributes like action and age but omits most actors and contains likely incorrect specifics such as a carried bag and a described carrying method.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extractor lists on the road and a pedestrian crossing but fails to capture the varied side-of-road relations for many people and mislabels the crossing when ground truth indicates jaywalking.
justifications.overall: The output is sparse and partially incorrect with many missing agents and some hallucinated attributes, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only 2 of 7 ground-truth persons and 1 of 5 vehicles, missing the majority of actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute types (actions, context, transport, age, vehicle states) but multiple labels contradict the ground truth and several actors are entirely omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only two spatial relations are provided and vehicle spatial relations are empty, failing to represent positions or relations for most detected actors.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and frequently inaccurate compared to ground truth, offering limited utility for reliable driving-scene perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all annotated persons and vehicles despite their clear presence in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few scene-level details (cones, white line, building signage) but misses critical semantic entities and attributes such as multiple pedestrians and vehicle motion states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some coarse spatial cues (cones left, white line right, buildings right) but lacks spatial relations involving pedestrians and vehicles present in the frame.
justifications.overall: Overall the output captures minimal static scene context but fails on core detection and attribute extraction required for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected none of the five pedestrians and both vehicles from the ground truth, so extraction is almost entirely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides only a generic scene description and no object attributes (actions, ages, motion status), so semantic detail is very limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some coarse scene-level spatial cues (cones on the left, buildings on both sides) but fails to report spatial relations of pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Because almost all objects and important attributes are missing despite minimal scene cues, overall extraction quality is very low.

justifications.completeness: Many people and multiple parked vehicles annotated in the ground truth are completely missing from the extraction.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction describes road markings and roadside objects but omits nearly all object-level attributes such as person actions, ages, and vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some left-right relations are reported for cones and the fence, but spatial relations for the numerous pedestrians and vehicles are absent, limiting spatial understanding.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few coarse scene elements but fails to report the numerous pedestrians and vehicles and most semantic details, yielding poor overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of annotated entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes for the single detected pedestrian (action, context, transporting, age) with some alignment to ground truth but overall many entities and attributes are missing or ambiguous.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single coarse spatial relation ('on the right side of the road') is provided and it does not cover or accurately reflect the multiple annotated pedestrian and vehicle positions.
justifications.overall: Low overall due to poor entity coverage and limited, partially correct semantic and spatial details.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds one pedestrian and the parked truck but misses two additional persons and associated annotations, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It includes useful attributes (motion status, trunk/doors, pedestrian action, scene description) but omits multiple instances and mislabels a senior as an adult.
justifications.spatial_relations: It provides a right-side relation for the detected pedestrian and a scene-level left/right remark, but spatial relations are missing or inconsistent for other pedestrians and the vehicle.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially correct for object states and scene cues but is incomplete in instance coverage and inconsistent in age and spatial details.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of five pedestrians and the single vehicle (2 of 6 ground-truth entities), yielding about 0.33 completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields for the detected objects (actions, context, age, doors/trunk) but omits most pedestrians and several contextual/transporting details, so moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a right-side relation for one pedestrian but omits spatial info for the vehicle and other pedestrians and does not reliably map simple context to precise spatial relations, so low score.
justifications.overall: Average of the metrics reflecting reasonable attribute detail for detected objects but poor coverage of pedestrians and inconsistent spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 pedestrian out of 6 annotated persons, so most subjects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided multiple attribute fields (action, context, age, spatial_relations) for the detected subject but missed communicative information present in the ground truth for another person and failed to report five other people.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives a plausible spatial relation for the single detected pedestrian but omits spatial relations for the majority of persons and does not capture varied contexts like 'exiting a building'.
justifications.overall: Captures one correct pedestrian with reasonable attributes but is largely incomplete, missing most people and some communicative cues, resulting in limited utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 1 of 5 annotated persons, missing four other people and their attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures a correct atomic action and one simple context but omits transporting, communicative cues, and multiple distinct person instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a left-side relation for one person which is partially correct but fails to report other spatial contexts like entering a building or walking on the road for other people.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction correctly labels a single pedestrian and some basic context but is largely incomplete and lacks important semantic details across most annotated persons.

justifications.completeness: Partially complete: the scene_context mentions a person pushing a cart but the extraction fails to include the annotated person and the parked vehicle in the structured pedestrians and vehicles lists.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate semantic content in freeform description (cones, person pushing a cart) but missing structured attributes from the ground truth such as age and vehicle motion status.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides useful left/right relations for cones and the person, but lacks spatial information for the annotated parked vehicle and no precise object-level localization.
justifications.overall: Overall limited by missing structured detections for the ground-truth person and vehicle despite some useful contextual and spatial cues in the scene description.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 vehicles and no pedestrians despite the ground truth containing one person and seven vehicles, missing most objects and attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some semantic labels are present (vehicle type, motion status, trunk/door states for one vehicle) but many attributes are omitted and the single pedestrian and age/action details are entirely missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides specific relations for the two reported vehicles which are plausible, but spatial coverage is limited to only those objects and lacks broader scene relations.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct facts but is incomplete and missing key objects and annotations, yielding low practical utility.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses the single pedestrian and the majority of vehicles (reports 2 of 7), so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial relations) but several fields are null and many objects are omitted, reducing overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Explicit spatial relations are provided for the two detected vehicles, which is helpful, but coverage is limited and relative positioning cannot be fully verified against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The output contains some correct semantic details but significant omissions (missing pedestrian and most vehicles) and at least one motion-label mismatch limit its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Misses the single annotated pedestrian and six of seven annotated vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic vehicle attributes for one object but omits pedestrian attributes and most vehicle instances and details.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers a plausible relation on the left side of the road for one vehicle but lacks relations for other objects and cannot be fully verified against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Detects one parked vehicle with some attributes but fails to report pedestrians and the majority of vehicles, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses the single pedestrian and most vehicles, reporting only two vehicles instead of six, so very incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides object types and some attributes but lacks many vehicle attributes, contains nulls for trunk/doors, and incorrectly labels a parked vehicle as stopped.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes simple spatial relations like left side and in front but relations are limited, not tied to ground-truth ids, and may be imprecise.
justifications.overall: Overall low quality due to the missed pedestrian, incomplete vehicle coverage, and some incorrect attributes despite a few useful type and relation entries.

justifications.completeness: Misses all pedestrians and two of three vehicles, only detects a single parked truck with basic status.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides minimal semantics (vehicle status and a type) and omits pedestrian actions, ages, and multiple vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a single spatial relation for one vehicle but lacks spatial descriptions for pedestrians and other vehicles, reducing overall spatial coverage.
justifications.overall: Low overall quality due to missing pedestrians and most vehicles despite a few correct vehicle attributes.

justifications.completeness: Misses one pedestrian and one vehicle present in the ground truth, so multiple objects are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes (actions, context, age, door/trunk states) for detected entities but omits the second person and does not include complex contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a plausible 'on the sidewalk' relation for the detected pedestrian but leaves vehicle relations empty and does not capture relations for the missing person.
justifications.overall: Partly accurate for detected objects with useful attributes, but missing entities and some contextual labels reduce the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses both pedestrians and one of two vehicles, only reporting a single parked truck.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle-level attributes and a scene description but omits person actions and one vehicle, reducing semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a correct relation for the reported truck but lacks relations for the missing vehicle and for pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate for the reported vehicle but major omissions of pedestrians and one vehicle limit overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all annotated persons and the parked vehicle, providing only a generic scene label and thus covers only a small fraction of required entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides minimal semantic detail (a short scene description) and omits actions, ages, and vehicle motion attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The output contains no explicit spatial relations between road users or the parked vehicle and only a vague description of buildings, failing to capture annotated relations like walking along the side or parked.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is largely incomplete and lacks the semantic and relational details required by the ground truth, meriting a low aggregate score.

justifications.completeness: 'The model failed to detect the two pedestrians from the ground truth and reported only a bicycle, so entity coverage is very incomplete.'
justifications.semantic_richness: 'The extraction includes a few attributes (motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial_relations) but omits key semantics like person actions, ages, and correct vehicle identification, yielding limited richness.'
justifications.spatial_relations: 'It correctly places a bicycle on the left side of the road, but omits spatial relations for the two people and the parked vehicle, so spatial information is only partially complete.'
justifications.overall: 'Overall the output shows some correct local information but misses critical entities and attributes from the ground truth, resulting in low overall quality.'

justifications.completeness: Very incomplete: it fails to report the single annotated pedestrian and the parked vehicle, capturing only generic scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Low semantic richness: while it gives a brief scene description, it omits entity types and attributes such as a walking adult and a parked vehicle.
justifications.spatial_relations: Limited spatial relations: it mentions a blue cart on the left and a center line but misses the pedestrian's location along the side of the road and the vehicle's parked status or position.
justifications.overall: Overall poor performance due to missing both annotated actors and their attributes despite a modest scene description.

justifications.completeness: Detected and described the pedestrian accurately but failed to report the parked vehicle present in ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured several person attributes and a descriptive scene phrase but omitted any vehicle information from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided a spatial relation for the pedestrian as on the road which is less precise than the ground truth indication of walking along the side of the road and no relations for the vehicle.
justifications.overall: Accurate person-level semantics but missed vehicle detection and gave a slightly imprecise spatial relation, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all annotated pedestrians and vehicles from the ground truth despite many being present, so it is almost completely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a terse scene label and two static objects (bike rack and building) are provided with no object counts, attributes, or actions, yielding very low semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: The output includes coarse left/right cues (bike rack on the left, building on the right) and a crosswalk label but fails to capture spatial relationships among the many pedestrians and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Because the extraction largely fails to report nearly all relevant actors and their semantics despite a minimal correct scene label, the overall quality is very poor.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected only a single pedestrian and omitted all other persons and all vehicles present in the ground truth, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected pedestrian several attributes were given (action, context, age) but many entity-level attributes and numerous other actors with distinct attributes from the ground truth were missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation ('on the sidewalk') was provided while multiple pedestrians exhibit different spatial contexts including jaywalking and vehicles with motion states were not reported.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a small correct fragment of the scene but misses most actors, vehicle information, and many contextual attributes, resulting in low overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Extracted only two pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains many persons and several vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes several attribute fields but many values are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth (wrong ages, missing many communicative and transporting labels) and only a tiny subset of actors are described.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation was provided and spatial context for most actors is omitted, so spatial information is largely missing or under-specified.
justifications.overall: The extraction identifies the general scene and some attributes for two people but is largely incomplete and contains notable semantic errors, yielding poor overall utility for perception benchmarking.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects only one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth contains 14 persons and 4 vehicles, so it misses the vast majority of entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some attributes (action, age, doors/trunk status) but omits many contextual labels (crossing, jaywalking, standing, communicative behaviors) and incorrectly assigns transporting.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few coarse spatial relations are given (near a truck, on/in the road) that are partially plausible but sparse and not systematically aligned with the ground-truth annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall the output is sparse and only partially accurate for reported objects, failing to capture most entities and many important semantic details from the scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians while the ground truth lists many more and it missed all vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute types per person (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several values are incorrect or inconsistently formatted compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided, but they are sparse, reference unannotated vehicles, and are often inaccurate or inconsistent with the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to very poor coverage of pedestrians and missing vehicles, with additional attribute inaccuracies despite some semantic detail.

justifications.completeness: Misses all annotated persons and the moving vehicle, reporting no pedestrians or vehicles despite ground truth having two people and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides only a brief scene description and an incorrect train mention but no object attributes, actions, ages, or motion details from the annotations.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives a vague relation about a train on the right but fails to report spatial relations for the annotated people and vehicle, and the train claim is not supported by the annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction largely omits annotated objects and their attributes, offering minimal and partly incorrect context so it is largely inadequate relative to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses both annotated persons entirely and only partially covers the single vehicle, so it omits key objects present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM supplies multiple attributes (type, motion, trunk/doors, scene context) which increases richness, but several of these attributes are unsupported or contradicted by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation is provided for the vehicle but no relations are given for the two persons and the claimed right-side train/tunnel context is not corroborated by the annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall the output contains some descriptive detail but fails to detect annotated pedestrians and includes unsupported/possibly incorrect object labels and attributes, leading to low reliability.

justifications.completeness: Missed all annotated persons and the moving vehicle; only provided background scene description.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided some environmental details (bike lane, tunnel, walls) but no actor attributes (actions, age, motion) or object states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Described surrounding elements (walls, fence) but gave no spatial relationships between actors and scene elements or relative positions of objects.
justifications.overall: Largely failed to capture critical dynamic entities and their semantics, offering only limited static scene context.

justifications.completeness: Missed all annotated persons and the moving vehicle, providing only a generic road description.
justifications.semantic_richness: Gave some descriptive scene detail about road markings and a tunnel but omitted critical semantic entities and attributes like persons, ages, actions, and vehicle state.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes basic left/right road-marking relations and direction towards a tunnel but lacks spatial relations involving the annotated people and vehicle.
justifications.overall: Because it failed to detect any annotated persons or the vehicle despite reasonable scene description, the extraction is largely insufficient for driving-perception use.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omitted all detected actors despite ground truth containing two persons and one vehicle, so it is almost entirely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the scene description gives basic static context, it lacks nearly all semantic attributes from the ground truth such as actions, ages, and vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction reports some environmental spatial cues (bridge, fence on the right) but fails to locate or relate the persons and vehicle present in the frame.
justifications.overall: The output provides limited static scene detail but misses critical dynamic agents and their attributes, resulting in poor overall agreement with ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Very incomplete: it omits both person annotations (sitting and senior age) and related attributes, only reporting the bicycle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes several bicycle attributes and a descriptive scene sentence but misses key ground-truth semantics like both persons, their actions (sitting/biking) and ages, and even introduces an unsupported gender label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly associates the bicycle with the white wall with graffiti and mentions a nearby bench, but fails to encode the seated person's relation to the bench because persons are omitted.
justifications.overall: Low overall quality: bicycle detection and wall relation are reasonable, but omission of all person-level annotations and addition of unsupported detail produce poor fidelity to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Correctly identifies a person and the sitting action and non-communicative/transporting attributes but misses the ground truth simple context and mislabels age.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides additional scene description and multiple attributes beyond the person label, but includes incorrect age and an incorrect simple context which reduces reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible relations like against wall and on ground but misses the side of road relation from the ground truth and may overinterpret contact with the wall.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate extraction with correct action and many attributes but critical errors in simple context and age lower the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures key fields like atomic action, communicative, and transporting but mislabels age and disagrees with the ground truth simple context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides additional descriptive details such as a scene description and spatial relations that increase semantic content despite the incorrect age.
justifications.spatial_relations: Specifies relations like next to gravel and near concrete wall which add detail but these relations are not annotated in the ground truth and cannot be verified here.
justifications.overall: Partly accurate extraction with correct action and useful context but weakened by an age misclassification and unverifiable spatial assertions.

justifications.completeness: Detected the person, atomic action, and communicative/transporting absence but missed the ground-truth age (senior) and disagreed on Simple Context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a useful scene-level description and additional spatial detail beyond the ground truth but incorrectly labels age and introduces an unverified simple context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reported spatial relations are plausible and consistent with the described scene (next to curb, against concrete wall) even though the ground truth does not specify them.
justifications.overall: Informative and largely accurate for action and scene details but penalized for the incorrect age label and the Simple Context disagreement.

justifications.completeness: Extraction completely omits the single annotated person (senior sitting) and therefore misses required entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Contains a basic scene description but provides none of the ground-truth semantic attributes such as person presence, action, or age.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships or localization of the annotated person are provided, so spatial information is absent.
justifications.overall: Critical failure to detect and describe the annotated person makes the extraction largely incorrect despite a minor correct scene description.

justifications.completeness: The model failed to detect the single annotated person (a senior sitting), so it missed all required actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a basic scene description but omits all specific semantic attributes from the ground truth such as the person's presence, age, and action.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations or locations for the annotated person are provided, and no objects were detected to form relations.
justifications.overall: Because the single ground-truth person was entirely missed despite a correct road description, the overall usefulness of the extraction is very low.
